title: Mouthwatering Flaky Saudi Arabian Chicken Flatbread
description: A quick-fix Saudi-Arabian dish featuring panang curry paste and miso-marinate, this low-glycemic-friendly flatbread is perfect for a weeknight dinner. The combination of sour and flaky flavors will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the almond flour, salt, and black pepper.
In a separate bowl, mix together the miso paste, panang curry paste, lemon juice, and coconut oil.
Add the chicken pieces to the miso-curry mixture and marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Add the eggs and water to the almond flour mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough into a large circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Place the marinated chicken pieces in the center of the dough circle.
Fold the dough over the chicken to form a flatbread shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Brush the top of the flatbread with a little coconut oil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
